sections.title: Test Coverage Analysis
sections.content:
Based on my analysis of the codebase (~13,173 lines across 65 TypeScript files) with only 11 test files covering 14 test suites, here are the significant coverage gaps:

---

sections.title: **1. Model Router (`model-router.ts`) - COMPLETELY UNTESTED**
**2. GOAP Planner (`goap-planner.ts`) - COMPLETELY UNTESTED**
**3. Workflow Engine (`workflow-engine.ts`) - COMPLETELY UNTESTED**
**4. Task Router (`task-router.ts`) - COMPLETELY UNTESTED**
**5. Health Monitor (`health-monitor.ts`) - COMPLETELY UNTESTED**
**6. Vector Memory (`vector-memory.ts`) - COMPLETELY UNTESTED**
**7. Auth Middleware (`auth-middleware.ts`) - SECURITY CRITICAL, UNTESTED**
**8. Guardrails (`guardrails/builtins.ts`) - SECURITY CRITICAL, UNTESTED**
**9. Stats Engine (`stats-engine.ts`) - UNTESTED**
sections.content:
**Missing Coverage:**
- Timeline bucketing algorithm
- Peak concurrency calculation
- Tool/profile breakdown aggregation

---

sections.title: **10. Integration Test Gaps**
sections.content:
**Missing End-to-End Scenarios:**
- Multi-agent coordination with message passing
- Workflow → GOAP → Agent execution pipeline
- Memory tier promotion after agent completion
- Routing decision → Agent spawn → Outcome recording loop
- Checkpoint save → Crash → Resume flow
- Health monitor → Stale agent cleanup
- Auth failure → 401 response → retry with valid token

---

sections.title: Summary Statistics
sections.content:
| Category | Files | Tested | Untested | Coverage % |
|----------|-------|--------|----------|------------|
| Core Logic | 65 | 11 | 54 | 17% |
| Security Critical | 4 | 0 | 4 | 0% |
| AI/Planning | 5 | 0 | 5 | 0% |
| Persistence | 8 | 3 | 5 | 37.5% |
| **TOTAL** | **~65** | **~11** | **~54** | **~17%** |

**Priority Order (by risk):**
1. **Security** - auth-middleware, guardrails (credential leaks)
2. **Core Planning** - goap-planner, workflow-engine (business logic)
3. **Routing** - task-router, model-router (performance/cost)
4. **Memory** - vector-memory (data integrity)
5. **Monitoring** - health-monitor, stats-engine (observability)
